headline: JPMorgan Smart Monitor Cuts Research Time 83% for Investment Teams
the_so_what: An 83% reduction in research time for investment teams is a structural compression of one of wealth management's most expensive workflows. When Smart Monitor, Connect Coach, and AI-curated guidelines ship together, JPMorgan will have the first firm-wide AI productivity architecture across research, advisory, and compliance — a three-layer stack that rivals are building piecemeal. Goldman has 46,000 employees on its AI platform but focused on code and document generation. JPMorgan is targeting the advisor-specific workflow.
company_name: JPMorgan Private Bank and Wealth Management
source_name: JPMorgan Chase 2025 Investor Day — Asset & Wealth Management (slide 12)
summary: At JPMorgan Chase's 2025 Investor Day on May 20, slide 12 of the Asset & Wealth Management presentation disclosed Smart Monitor — an AI-integrated research platform for investors and portfolio teams built on the Spectrum platform. Smart Monitor provides AI-integrated workflow, automated insights, custom investment universes, and extensive market coverage. The result: an 83% reduction in time spent researching a topic, measured before vs. after deployment. The tool sits alongside Connect Coach (for advisors) and Guidelines (for operations) as one of three production AI platforms disclosed in the Investor Day presentation. All three reflect JPMorgan's stated objective of deploying AI to 'drive productivity,' 'increase revenue,' and — newly added for 2025 — 'enable quicker decision-making' and 'proactive and anticipatory client advice.'

key_stat.label: Reduction in time spent researching a topic — JPMorgan Smart Monitor (Spectrum), 2025 Investor Day

landscape_context.peer_comparison: Morgan Stanley (scaled, 98% adoption), BofA (scaled, 30B interactions), Goldman (deployed, 46K employees)
